name: Phase 0 — Hotfix: HACS scheduler reads nested schedule object
description: Single-story emergency fix for the v0.3 HACS release. The Python integration's schedule applier reads schedule fields one level too high in the JSON returned by GET /api/v1/schedules — `hvac.get('high_temps')` instead of `hvac['schedule']['high_temps']`. Result: the cache is always empty, the slot-bounds check at scheduler.py:127 skips every apply, and no user has ever had a setpoint applied by the integration. Fix the path, add a regression test that fails on the old shape and passes on the correct one. Tag a v0.3.x patch release immediately. This is the absolute prerequisite for any other work — until this ships, nothing else matters.
env_policy: Python integration code lives at custom_components/hungry_machines/. Verify with pytest against the existing conftest.py + happy-dom-style aiohttp mocks. No live API calls. No env vars required. Tests must pass with empty .env / no Supabase access.

stories.title: Read HVAC schedule from the nested 'schedule' object, not the top-level appliance entry
stories.description: As the HACS integration, I must correctly destructure the GET /api/v1/schedules response so that `apply_current_slot` actually applies setpoints. Today the cache is always empty and apply silently no-ops.
acceptanceCriteria: Read `custom_components/hungry_machines/scheduler.py:79-100` end to end first. The bug is at lines 94-99: `hvac.get('high_temps')` and `hvac.get('low_temps')` reach into the appliance entry directly instead of the nested `schedule` sub-object. Replace lines 94-99 with: `sched = hvac.get('schedule') or {}` followed by `cached = {'high_temps': list(sched.get('high_temps') or []), 'low_temps': list(sched.get('low_temps') or []), 'fetched_at': datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()}`. Do not change anything else in this file.

Create `custom_components/hungry_machines/tests/test_scheduler.py` (the directory exists; check whether the file already exists and extend it if so, else create). Add a fixture that builds a realistic /api/v1/schedules body matching the documented contract: `{'date': '2026-05-06', 'appliances': [{'appliance_id': 'a1', 'appliance_type': 'hvac', 'name': 'AC', 'schedule': {'intervals': list(range(48)), 'high_temps': [74.0]*48, 'low_temps': [70.0]*48}, 'savings_pct': 18.5, 'source': 'optimization'}]}`. Mock `aiohttp_client.async_get_clientsession` (or the request directly via `unittest.mock.patch`) so `session.get(...).__aenter__().json()` returns this body and `.status` is 200.

Test 1 — happy path: call `await scheduler.fetch_today_schedule(hass, entry)` against the realistic body. Assert the returned cache dict has `len(cache['high_temps']) == 48`, `len(cache['low_temps']) == 48`, `cache['high_temps'][0] == 74.0`, `cache['low_temps'][0] == 70.0`, and that `hass.data[DOMAIN]['schedule']` was set to the same object. This test must FAIL on master before the fix and PASS after.

Test 2 — old (buggy) flat shape regression marker: build a body where high_temps/low_temps are at the *appliance* level (not nested under `schedule`). Call `fetch_today_schedule`. Assert `len(cache['high_temps']) == 0` (because the new code only reads from `hvac['schedule']`, so a flat shape correctly yields empty arrays — this documents that we no longer support the broken legacy shape).

Test 3 — apply_current_slot integration: with the cache populated from Test 1, set `entry.options[CONF_CLIMATE_ENTITY] = 'climate.test'`, mock `hass.services.async_call`, freeze time to a known half-hour boundary (e.g. 14:00 → slot 28), call `await scheduler.apply_current_slot(hass, entry)`, assert `hass.services.async_call` was awaited exactly once with service_domain='climate', service='set_temperature', and data containing `target_temp_high == 74.0` and `target_temp_low == 70.0` and `entity_id == 'climate.test'`. This is the end-to-end proof that a correct schedule actually moves the thermostat.

Verify: `cd hungry-machines_base-frontend && pytest custom_components/hungry_machines/tests/test_scheduler.py -v` — all three tests pass. Also `cd hungry-machines_base-frontend && pytest` (full Python suite) shows no regressions. Also `grep -F "hvac.get('schedule')" custom_components/hungry_machines/scheduler.py | wc -l` returns at least 1 (the fixed line). Also `grep -E "hvac\.get\(['\"]high_temps" custom_components/hungry_machines/scheduler.py | wc -l` returns 0 (the bug is gone).

Bump the integration version: edit `custom_components/hungry_machines/manifest.json` and increment the patch version (e.g. 0.3.3 → 0.3.4). Add a one-line entry to README.md or a CHANGELOG note describing 'Fix: schedule applier was caching empty arrays, no setpoints were ever applied (regression from v0.3.0).'
stories.notes: scheduler.py:94 fixed — `sched = hvac.get('schedule') or {}` then read high_temps/low_temps off `sched`. test_scheduler.py extended with three new cases covering the realistic nested body, the legacy flat-shape regression marker (yields empty arrays), and an end-to-end fetch→apply at slot 28. All 22 Python tests pass; all 92 vitest cases still pass. manifest.json + package.json bumped to 0.3.4 and a one-line v0.3.4 changelog entry added to README. Files changed: custom_components/hungry_machines/scheduler.py, custom_components/hungry_machines/tests/test_scheduler.py, custom_components/hungry_machines/manifest.json, package.json, README.md.
